title: Beacon Documentation
body: Welcome to the Beacon documentation. Beacon is an HTTP monitoring library for .NET that helps you track response times, error rates, and availability.

title: Setup (Moved)
body: This page has moved to Getting Started .

title: API Reference
body: Core Types Type Description HttpMonitor Main monitoring class MonitorResult Result of a monitoring check AlertRule Defines when alerts trigger MonitorOptions Configuration options Key Methods public class HttpMonitor { public Task StartAsync ( CancellationToken ct = default ) ; public Task StopAsync ( CancellationToken ct = default ) ; public Task < MonitorResult > CheckAsync ( CancellationToken ct = default ) ; } For setup instructions, see Getting Started . For configuration, see Configuration .

title: v3.2.0
body: What's New in v3.2 Added MonitorGroup for managing related monitors Improved retry logic with exponential backoff New Slack alert integration Performance improvements: 30% faster health checks

title: Getting Started
body: Installation dotnet add package Beacon Create Your First Monitor using Beacon ; var monitor = new HttpMonitor ( " https://api.example.com/health " ) ; monitor . Interval = TimeSpan . FromMinutes ( 1 ) ; monitor . OnFailure += ( sender , e ) => Console . WriteLine ( $" Alert: { e . StatusCode } at { e . Timestamp } " ) ; await monitor . StartAsync ( ) ; Note Beacon requires .NET 9 or later. See the Configuration guide for retry policies and alert thresholds.

title: Installation
body: Package Manager dotnet add package Beacon From Source Clone the repository and build: git clone https://github.com/example/beacon.git cd beacon dotnet build Verify Installation using Beacon ; Console . WriteLine ( $" Beacon version: { BeaconInfo . Version } " ) ; Return to the Getting Started guide for next steps.

title: Configuration
body: Monitor Configuration builder . Services . AddBeacon ( options => { options . DefaultInterval = TimeSpan . FromMinutes ( 5 ) ; options . AlertThreshold = 3 ; options . TimeoutMs = 5000 ; } ) ; { " Beacon " : { " DefaultInterval " : " 00:05:00 " , " AlertThreshold " : 3 , " TimeoutMs " : 5000 } } Warning Setting TimeoutMs below 1000 may cause false positives on slow networks. Alert Configuration Alerts can be sent via email, Slack, or custom webhook: options . Alerts . Add ( new SlackAlert ( " #ops-alerts " , webhookUrl ) ) ; options . Alerts . Add ( new EmailAlert ( " oncall@example.com " ) ) ; See the API Reference for all alert types.

title: Migrating from v2 to v3
body: Rename BeaconClient to HttpMonitor The main class has been renamed: // Old (v2) var client =new BeaconClient ( "https://api.example.com" ); // New (v3) var monitor =new HttpMonitor ( "https://api.example.com" ); Update Configuration builder . Services . AddBeacon ( options => { options . DefaultInterval = TimeSpan . FromMinutes ( 5 ) ; options . AlertThreshold = 3 options . TimeoutMs = 5000 options . EnableMetrics = true ; } ) ; Remove Deprecated Calls // This method is removed in v3 monitor . PollOnce (); // Use the async version instead await monitor . CheckAsync (); Warning The PollOnce() method has been removed with no synchronous replacement. All monitoring operations are now async. Tip Run the Beacon migration analyzer: dotnet tool install Beacon.Migrator to find all deprecated API usage automatically. Note The v2 configuration format is still supported but will be removed in v4.

title: Pipeline Configuration
body: Pipeline Overview Beacon uses a pipeline model for processing monitoring results. Configuration Configure your monitoring pipeline in Program.cs : builder . Services . AddBeacon ( options => { options . Pipeline . AddStep < ValidationStep > ( ) . AddStep < ThrottlingStep > ( ) . AddStep < AlertingStep > ( ) ; } ) ; Pipeline Steps Each step processes the monitoring result and can modify, filter, or trigger actions.
